entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop species being improved for yield and adaptability under specific cultivation conditions

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, a key productivity metric

entities.name: ADAPTABILITY
entities.description: The ability of the crop to perform and survive under specific environmental conditions

entities.name: DIRECT-SEEDED CULTIVATION
entities.description: A farming method where seeds are sown directly into the field rather than transplanted

entities.name: KASP
entities.description: Kompetitive Allele-Specific PCR, a genotyping method used to develop molecular markers

entities.name: KASP MARKERS
entities.description: Molecular markers developed using the KASP method to identify specific traits or genes

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach that utilizes genomic information and molecular markers to select desired traits

entities.name: PYRAMIDING
entities.description: The process of combining multiple favorable genes into a single genotype

entities.name: BIOTIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to resist biological stressors such as pests and diseases

entities.name: ANAEROBIC GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of seeds to germinate successfully in low-oxygen or flooded conditions

entities.name: ROOT TRAITS
entities.description: Physical and functional characteristics of the plant root system

entities.name: LODGING RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to remain upright and resist falling over

entities.name: EARLY-UNIFORM EMERGENCE
entities.description: The trait of seeds emerging from the soil simultaneously and at an early stage

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used for genetic validation and comparison

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci, regions of DNA associated with a particular phenotypic trait
